name: Halo: The Master Chief Collection
post_apply_message:
Halo MCC + AlphaRing — local SPLITSCREEN across all MCC games.

Installed: AlphaRing's proxy DLL as WTSAPI32.dll into
    <game>/MCC/Binaries/Win64/          (note the CASE — it matters)
plus the launch option WINEDLLOVERRIDES="WTSAPI32=n,b" %command%.
Both match the author's own video (Jack Bittner, M26Mzan8SNM) exactly.

NOW TEST IT: launch MCC and press F4 (or Controller Back + Start). If the
AlphaRing menu opens, you're done — use its Bind buttons to assign each
controller to a player.

TRY ANTI-CHEAT ON FIRST. Until 2026-07-20 this recipe installed the DLL to
a lowercase 'mcc/binaries/win64' — a DIFFERENT directory on Linux — so the
mod was never beside the exe and could not load in EITHER anti-cheat mode.
Every previous conclusion about EAC came from that broken state, so it is
all untested now. Anti-cheat ON is the mode that actually launches, so try
it first.

If F4 does nothing with anti-cheat ON, then EAC really is blocking the DLL
and it needs anti-cheat OFF — which on this Deck refuses to launch even
with a stock game folder. The lead there is to bypass MCC's launcher and
run MCC/Binaries/Win64/MCC-Win64-Shipping.exe directly (the root launcher
is mcclauncher.exe), which is what anti-cheat-disabled does anyway.

Recipe pins AlphaRing 1.3.3 by sha256 (00474043…). kirklandsig and
megabitt01 are collaborators, not rival forks — no better build to chase.
notes:
Built 2026-07-13. Halo MCC = Steam-native (permanent appid 976730), so no non-Steam shortcut / gospel pin — its prefix restores by 976730 automatically. AlphaRing (github megabitt01/AlphaRing, fork of wouter51/AlphaRing) is a single-DLL modding tool: it ships as WTSAPI32.dll, a proxy for the Windows WTSAPI32 system DLL, dropped into mcc/binaries/win64 next to MCC-Win64-Shipping.exe. Install per its README: place the DLL, add WINEDLLOVERRIDES="WTSAPI32=n,b" %command% on Linux/Deck, launch with EAC disabled, toggle menu with F4 / Controller Back+Start. Purpose: PC splitscreen for all MCC titles + Halo 3 camera/object browsing; this fork adds controller-to-player Bind buttons and fixes default gamepad mapping (new profiles init with standard Xbox Halo controls). Ref video: youtube M26Mzan8SNM ('How To Play Halo Splitscreen on PC | AlphaRing Mod for MCC'). DLL fetched as a remote_payload (6.1MB, not committed) rather than into git. EAC-disabled is a manual in-launcher choice we can't force from a file step; flagged in post_apply_message. Untested on Tony's Deck as of build.

BLOCKED ON HARDWARE 2026-07-20 — NOT OUR BUG. Tony tested: MCC launches fine WITH anti-cheat, and refuses to launch with ANTI-CHEAT DISABLED. First suspicion was AlphaRing's proxy DLL, since EAC blocks it when enabled and loads it when disabled — exactly the difference between the two cases. Ruled out by reverting the recipe: with a STOCK, unmodified game folder, anti-cheat-disabled still refuses to launch.

So the EAC-disabled launch path is broken on this Deck independently of us, and AlphaRing never gets a chance to load. AlphaRing REQUIRES EAC off (EAC will not load an injected/proxy DLL), so this recipe cannot work until that launch path does. Nothing to fix in the recipe itself — the copy_files + launch_options steps applied correctly (log 11:33:45).

RELATED KNOWN ISSUE: 343/Valve documented a Steam Deck bug where selecting an anti-cheat launch option launches the OPPOSITE one. MCC's launch-option handling on Deck is unreliable in its own right, which is the likeliest neighbourhood for this.

NEXT LEAD (untested): anti-cheat-disabled just runs mcc/binaries/win64/MCC-Win64-Shipping.exe directly. If we can launch that exe inside MCC's own prefix we bypass the broken selector entirely — the usual trick is a launch option that rewrites the exe in %command%. Needs the exact default exe name from the live shortcut before it's worth writing as a step.

DO NOT re-litigate the fork question on this evidence: kirklandsig vs megabitt01 is irrelevant while no build can load at all.

BOTH BRANCHES TESTED 2026-07-20 — EAC REQUIREMENT CONFIRMED. Tony ran it both ways: WITH anti-cheat the game launches but EAC BLOCKS the DLL (no AlphaRing menu on F4); WITHOUT anti-cheat the game refuses to start at all, even with a stock unmodified folder. So the 'must disable EAC' note was a correct inference, not an invention — but it also means the mod cannot work until the EAC-disabled launch path works.

VERIFIED CORRECT against the author's own video (Jack Bittner, youtube M26Mzan8SNM): the download is megabitt01/AlphaRing (what we pin) and the Linux launch option is WINEDLLOVERRIDES="WTSAPI32=n,b" %command% (what we write), character for character. The video credits WinterSquire, wouter51, megabitt01 AND kirklandsig together — they are collaborators, so there is no 'better fork' to switch to. Nothing about our install is wrong.

THE ONE REMAINING LEAD: anti-cheat-disabled does nothing clever — it just runs mcc/binaries/win64/MCC-Win64-Shipping.exe instead of going through the launcher. If a launch option rewrites the exe in %command% to that binary, we get EAC-off behaviour AND skip whatever is broken in MCC's launcher/selector on Deck. Needs the game root's launcher exe name before it can be written; the substitution form is:
    bash -c 'exec "${@/<launcher>.exe/mcc/binaries/win64/MCC-Win64-Shipping.exe}"' -- %command%
(Note the game folder really is lowercase mcc/binaries/win64 on Linux — confirmed by the copy_files log line at 11:33:45.)

If that fails too, park this like FUEL. Everything under our control is provably correct; the failure is in MCC's own Deck launch path.

*** ROOT CAUSE FOUND 2026-07-20 — WRONG CASE, WRONG DIRECTORY. ***
The 🔬 Diagnostics dump exposed it immediately. The game root contains BOTH 'mcc' and 'MCC' as SEPARATE directories, and the only shipping binary is MCC/Binaries/Win64/MCC-Win64-Shipping.exe (67MB, uppercase). This recipe had every path in lowercase, so copy_files put AlphaRing's WTSAPI32.dll into mcc/binaries/win64/ — a different directory on a case-sensitive filesystem, nowhere near the exe. Log line 11:33:45 shows it plainly. A proxy DLL only loads from the executable's OWN folder, so the mod could never have worked, with or without anti-cheat.

THAT MEANS THE EARLIER CONCLUSION WAS WRONG. 'EAC blocks the DLL' was inferred from 'no menu with anti-cheat on' — but there was no DLL beside the exe to block. Whether EAC actually blocks it is now UNTESTED, and the with-anti-cheat path is worth retrying first since that one at least launches.

Fixed: copy target and marker_files are now the real uppercase paths, plus a remove_files step that clears the stray from the phantom lowercase tree so a broken install heals on re-apply. Lesson: paths written from memory at build time are a guess; ext4 does not forgive case, and Windows/Proton testing hides it because NTFS does.
